title: Authorization / Access Denied
description: The user is missing one or more authorization objects required to perform the action. This is the most common support issue in SAP.
steps: Run SU53 immediately as the affected user (or ask them to run it) right after the error occurs — SU53 only stores the most recent failed check.
Note the missing authorization object, field name, and the value that was checked.
Open PFCG and locate the role(s) assigned to the user. Search for the missing authorization object on the Authorization tab.
Add or correct the field value in the role and regenerate the authorization profile.
Re-assign the role in SU01 if it was newly added — profile regeneration alone is usually sufficient for existing assignments.
Ask the user to re-test. If SU53 shows a new failure, repeat for each additional missing object.

title: User Account Locked
description: A user cannot log in because their account is locked, either administratively or due to repeated failed logon attempts.
steps: Go to SU01, enter the username, and open the record in Change mode.
On the Logon Data tab, check the lock status icon and the reason for the lock.
If locked by failed logon attempts, clear the lock and save.
If administratively locked by another admin, investigate the reason before unlocking.
Advise the user to use a new password after unlocking, especially if the lock was due to multiple failed attempts.
Check SM21 system log for the pattern of logon failures if this recurs.

title: Transport Imported but Change Not Visible
description: A transport was imported successfully but the expected changes are not visible in the target system.
steps: Open STMS and check the import log for the transport in the target system's import queue — a green traffic light does not mean error-free.
Verify the correct client was targeted (Customizing transports are client-specific).
Open the transport in SE09 and check the object list — confirm the expected object is included.
Check whether another transport imported after this one overwrote the same object.
If the import log shows errors or warnings, re-import the transport from the STMS queue.
For ABAP programs, check if the program needs to be activated after import.

title: OData Service Not Found / Not Active
description: An OData service returns a 404 or is not accessible. Usually caused by the service not being activated on the Gateway or a stale metadata cache.
steps: Go to /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and search for the service by name. Check whether it is active.
If not found, add and activate the service, setting the correct system alias.
Check SICF to ensure the ICF node for the service path is active.
Run /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP on the backend to clear the metadata cache.
Run /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP on the Gateway frontend.
Test the $metadata URL in a browser to confirm the service is responding.

title: Background Job Failed or Aborted
description: A scheduled background job has failed, aborted, or did not run as expected.
steps: Open SM37 and filter by job name, user, or date range. Identify the failed job.
Open the job log to read the last message — it usually points to the root cause.
If the log references an ABAP exception or short dump, go to ST22 to find the dump.
Check SM21 for system-level errors at the time of failure.
Identify whether the issue is a data problem, missing authorization (check SU53), or program error.
Fix the root cause and reschedule or re-run the job.

title: ABAP Runtime Error / Short Dump
description: An ABAP program terminated with a runtime error. Common causes include type conflicts, null object access, or missing configuration data.
steps: Go to ST22 and find the dump by date/time, user, or program name.
Read the exception description and the active call stack to identify where the error occurred.
Check whether it is a standard SAP program — if so, search SAP Notes (OSS) for the dump type.
If it is a custom program, involve the ABAP developer to fix the logic.
If the dump involves authorization checks, check SU53 for missing authorization objects.

title: FI Document Not Posting
description: A financial document cannot be posted. Common causes include closed posting periods, blocked G/L accounts, missing cost objects, or document balance errors.
steps: Note the exact error message — it usually contains the G/L account, cost center, or period causing the issue.
If the error is 'posting period closed', the fiscal period is locked. Ask FI admins to open the period in OB52.
If the G/L account is blocked for posting, check the account master data and remove the posting block.
If a cost center or cost object is missing or invalid, verify the CO assignment on the line item.
If the document does not balance to zero, check all debit and credit line items.
For recurring errors, check SM21 for update task failures after posting.

title: Payment Run Not Picking Up Open Items
description: The F110 automatic payment run is not selecting expected vendor invoices. Usually caused by incorrect payment parameters, blocked invoices, or missing payment method configuration.
steps: Open F110 and review the payment run parameters: company code, payment method, vendor selection, and due date.
Check FBL1N for the vendor's open items — confirm the invoice is open and not already cleared.
In FBL1N, check the payment block indicator on the invoice (a payment block prevents selection).
Confirm the payment method on the invoice matches what is configured in the payment run.
Check that the invoice due date falls within the next payment date range in F110.
Review the payment log from the last run for exclusion reasons.

title: Sales Order Blocked
description: A sales order has a delivery block, billing block, or credit block that prevents it from being processed further.
steps: Open VA03 and navigate to the header. Check the Delivery Block and Billing Block fields.
If there is a credit block, check the customer's credit status — the credit manager must release it.
For a delivery block, understand the reason (e.g., incomplete data, manual hold) and remove it in VA02.
For a billing block on a line item, check item conditions and pricing — blocks often indicate a pricing issue.
After removing blocks, check whether downstream documents (delivery, invoice) can now be created.

title: Goods Receipt Quantity or Value Mismatch
description: A goods receipt does not match the purchase order quantity or value, or stock levels are not updated as expected.
steps: Open MIGO and display the material document to see what was actually posted.
Compare the posted quantity and unit of measure against the PO line in ME23N.
Check MB51 for the material document history to see all movements for the material.
If a GR was posted with the wrong quantity, post a reversal in MIGO (movement type 102) and repost correctly.
Use MMBE to verify the current stock balance reflects the expected quantity.
If the GR is missing entirely, check SM37 for any automated GR jobs that may have failed.

title: Timesheet Entry Missing or Not Saved
description: A user's CATS timesheet entry is not visible or appears to not have been saved.
steps: Open CAT2 for the affected employee and date — check whether the entry exists and was saved and released.
Unsaved or unreleased entries will not transfer to HR. Look for entries in 'In Process' status.
Check SM37 for the CATS transfer job (RPTCAT** variants) to confirm it ran and completed successfully.
If the entry should have transferred to HR, use PT50 to check whether the absence/attendance is visible.
If entries are systematically missing, check the CATS transfer job schedule and last run log.

title: Workflow Stuck or Not Progressing
description: A workflow instance is not advancing, usually because a workitem has no assigned agent, the responsible user cannot action it, or the workflow hit an error.
steps: Go to SWI5 and search by task or status to find the stuck workitem.
Open the workitem and check whether an agent is assigned — no agent means no one is responsible.
Use SWIA to execute, forward, or complete the workitem administratively to unblock the process.
Check the workflow log (SWI1) to understand where and why the process stalled.
If no agent is assigned systematically, review the role resolution and org data in PPOME.

title: RFC Connection Failing
description: An RFC (Remote Function Call) to another SAP system or external service is failing. Causes include wrong connection parameters, the target system being unavailable, or authorization issues.
steps: Open SM59 and find the RFC destination by name. Use the Test Connection and Remote Login buttons to diagnose.
Check the hostname, system number, and logon credentials configured in the destination.
Test the network connection independently (ping / traceroute to the target host).
Check whether the target system is running — verify in the target system's SM21 or with the basis team.
For Trusted RFC connections, verify the trust settings are configured on both systems.
Check SM21 for RFC-related errors at the time of the failure.

title: Leave Quota Exceeded or Missing
description: An employee cannot book leave because their quota is exhausted, not yet accrued, or missing entirely.
steps: Open PT50 for the employee and check the absence quota balance for the relevant absence type.
If the quota is zero or missing, check PT60 to see when time evaluation last ran and whether it accrued the quota.
Verify the employee's employment status and working schedule — incorrect schedules prevent accrual.
If quota should have been generated but was not, re-run time evaluation in PT60 for the employee.
For manual quota corrections, an HR admin can add or adjust quotas directly via PA30 (infotype 2006).

title: IDoc Failed or Stuck in Error
description: An inbound or outbound IDoc failed during processing. Common causes include missing master data, partner profile issues, or application posting errors.
steps: Open WE02 and locate the failed IDoc by status, message type, partner, or time range.
Review the status records and error text to identify the actual reason for failure.
Use WE05 if you need broader filtering across message types or partners.
Fix the root cause before retrying the message.
Reprocess the IDoc in BD87.
Confirm the IDoc reaches a successful final status and the expected business document is created.

title: Update Task Failed / Data Not Saved
description: The transaction appeared to save, but the database update failed in the update task. This usually requires checking SM13 and related technical logs.
steps: Go to SM13 and search for failed update requests around the time of the issue.
Open the failed update request and review the error details.
If there is a related ABAP runtime error, inspect it in ST22.
Check SM21 for additional system-level information at the same timestamp.
Fix the underlying issue and repeat the business transaction if necessary.

title: Purchase Order Not Released
description: A purchase order is waiting for approval or not progressing through the release strategy.
steps: Open ME23N and check the current release status of the purchase order.
Verify whether the PO was changed after a previous approval, which may have reset the release.
Confirm the correct release strategy and approver are being determined.
Use ME29N to release the PO if you have the proper authority.
Re-display the document and confirm the release status has updated.

title: Vendor Invoice Blocked
description: A vendor invoice is blocked due to quantity or price variance, invoice verification rules, or payment restrictions.
steps: Find the invoice in FBL1N and confirm its current open and block status.
Review the invoice details in MIRO and compare them with the purchase order and goods receipt.
Resolve the underlying business variance first.
Release the blocked invoice in MRBR if appropriate.
Verify that the invoice is now eligible for payment.

title: Workflow Item Missing from Inbox
description: A workflow workitem exists or should exist, but the user cannot see it in their SAP inbox.
steps: Check SBWP to confirm the workitem is not already present in the user's inbox.
Use SWI5 to search for the workflow item by task, user, or status.
Verify whether an agent was assigned to the workitem.
If the item is stuck, use SWIA for administrative processing.
Ask the user to refresh the inbox and confirm visibility after the fix.

title: Material Missing or Not Extended
description: A material cannot be found in the expected organizational level or required view. The record may not exist or may not be extended to the plant or storage-relevant views.
steps: Open MM03 and check whether the material exists at all.
Verify whether the required plant or view is maintained for the material.
If the material exists but lacks required data, extend or update it in MM02.
If the material does not exist, create it in MM01 according to business rules.
Re-test the original process once the material master is complete.

title: Spool Output Missing or Not Printed
description: A report or form appears to have run, but the expected spool output is missing, stuck, or sent to the wrong device.
steps: Confirm the report or background job actually completed successfully.
Search SP01 for the spool request by user, program, or date.
Check the spool status and target output device.
Verify whether the output was sent to the correct printer or logical device.
Reprint or forward the existing spool request if appropriate.

title: Record Locked by Another User or Process
description: A business object or table entry is locked and cannot be changed. This may be a valid active lock or a stale lock left behind by a failed process.
steps: Search SM12 for the lock entry by user, object, or key.
Confirm whether the locking session is still active.
Check SM21 if the lock appears to be stale after a failure.
Ask the owning user or team to exit cleanly if possible.
Delete the lock only when you are sure it is no longer in active use.

title: qRFC Queue Stuck or Not Processing
description: A queued RFC message is stuck in outbound or inbound processing. Common causes include application errors, RFC issues, or dependent queues waiting to be cleared.
steps: Check SMQ1 in the source system to see whether the outbound queue is stuck.
Check SMQ2 in the target system to see whether the message arrived but is waiting there.
Review SLG1 for application-level error details.
Verify SM59 if the queue issue appears to be communication-related.
Fix the root cause before restarting or unlocking the queue.

title: Purchase Requisition Not Released
description: A purchase requisition is waiting for approval or not progressing through its release strategy.
steps: Open ME53N and review the release status of the requisition.
Check whether document changes reset the release status.
Confirm the correct release strategy and approver determination.
Use ME54N to release the requisition if you are authorized.
Re-display the requisition and confirm the updated release status.

title: Pricing Not Determined or Incorrect
description: A sales order has missing or incorrect pricing because the condition record, validity, or determination logic is not matching as expected.
steps: Open the sales order in VA03 and identify which pricing condition is missing or incorrect.
Check the sales area, customer, material, and pricing date on the order.
Use VK13 to verify that a valid condition record exists.
Confirm whether pricing procedure and access sequence logic are correct.
Re-price the order in VA02 after correcting the underlying issue.

title: Outbound Delivery Not Created
description: A sales order is not resulting in an outbound delivery because of blocks, missing confirmations, delivery relevance issues, or incorrect dates.
steps: Open VA03 and check the sales order status and any delivery blocks.
Review delivery-relevant dates, confirmed quantity, and schedule lines.
Try manual delivery creation in VL01N if appropriate.
Check whether a delivery already exists in VL03N.
If the delivery exists, continue processing in VL02N.

title: FI Posting Period Closed
description: A financial posting cannot be made because the accounting period is closed for the relevant company code or account type.
steps: Capture the posting date, company code, and account type from the error.
Check OB52 to confirm whether the period is open for that account type.
Verify that the posting date is correct and falls into the intended period.
Open the period only if you are authorized and business approval exists.
Repeat the posting once the period issue is resolved.

title: Employee Missing from Organizational Structure
description: An employee exists in HR master data but is not visible or correctly placed in the organizational structure.
steps: Check the employee's organizational assignment data in PA30.
Open PPOME to verify the org unit and position assignment.
Use PPOSE to confirm whether the employee appears in the expected structure.
Correct the org assignment or holder relationship if necessary.
Validate the final placement in both PPOME and PA20.

title: Leave Quota Missing or Incorrect
description: An employee's leave balance is missing, too low, or not updated because accrual or master data is incomplete or incorrect.
steps: Check the employee's quota balances in PT50.
Review or rerun PT60 if accrual should already have happened.
Check relevant HR master data in PA30.
Correct quota data in infotype 2006 if a manual adjustment is required.
Recheck the balance in PT50 after correction.

title: New Employee Setup Incomplete
description: A new employee was started in HR but required infotypes or organizational placement are incomplete, so downstream processes do not work correctly.
steps: Review the completed hiring action in PA40.
Check PA30 for missing or incomplete infotypes.
Verify organizational placement in PPOME or PPOSE.
Correct any missing data and perform a final validation in PA20.
Retest the affected downstream process after the employee record is complete.
